words.name: dup
words.description: Duplicate the top stack item.

words.name: drop
words.description: Discard the top stack item.

words.name: swap
words.description: Swap the top two stack items.

words.name: over
words.description: Copy the second stack item to the top.

words.name: rot
words.stackEffect: a b c -- b c a
words.description: Rotate the top three stack items.

words.name: swapd
words.stackEffect: a b c -- b a c
words.description: Swap the two items below the top stack item.

words.name: nip
words.description: Discard the second stack item and keep the top item.

words.name: tuck
words.description: Copy the top stack item beneath the next item.

words.name: pick
words.description: Copy the stack item at depth n to the top.

words.name: roll
words.description: Move the stack item at depth n to the top.

words.name: empty
words.description: Clear the entire data stack.
Add two numeric values.
Subtract the top value from the next value.
Multiply two numeric values.
Divide the next value by the top value.
Compute the truncating remainder of integer division.

words.name: mod
words.description: Compute Euclidean modulo, returning a non-negative result.

words.name: neg
words.description: Negate the top numeric value.

words.name: abs
words.description: Replace a number with its absolute value.

words.name: max
words.description: Keep the larger of two values.

words.name: min
words.description: Keep the smaller of two values.

words.name: sqrt
words.description: Compute the square root of a numeric value.

words.name: pow
words.description: Raise a numeric base to a numeric exponent.

words.name: exp
words.description: Compute e raised to a numeric value.

words.name: log
words.description: Compute the natural logarithm of a numeric value.

words.name: log10
words.description: Compute the base-10 logarithm of a numeric value.

words.name: sin
words.description: Compute the sine of a numeric value in radians.

words.name: cos
words.description: Compute the cosine of a numeric value in radians.

words.name: tan
words.description: Compute the tangent of a numeric value in radians.

words.name: floor
words.description: Round a numeric value down to an integer.

words.name: ceil
words.description: Round a numeric value up to an integer.

words.name: round
words.description: Round a numeric value to the nearest integer.

words.name: pred
words.description: Decrement an integer by one.

words.name: succ
words.description: Increment an integer by one.

words.name: square
words.description: Square an integer or float.

words.name: cube
words.description: Cube an integer or float.

words.name: pi
words.description: Push pi as a floating-point value.

words.name: e
words.description: Push Euler's number as a floating-point value.

words.name: tau
words.description: Push tau, equal to two pi, as a floating-point value.

words.name: inf
words.description: Push positive infinity as a floating-point value.

words.name: nan
words.description: Push NaN as a floating-point value.

words.name: inf?
words.description: Check whether the input is an infinite float.

words.name: nan?
words.description: Check whether the input is a NaN float.

words.name: rand
words.description: Push a random integer.

words.name: and
words.description: Logical AND for booleans, bitwise AND for integers.

words.name: or
words.description: Logical OR for booleans, bitwise OR for integers.

words.name: xor
words.description: Logical XOR for booleans, bitwise XOR for integers.

words.name: not
words.description: Logical NOT for booleans, bitwise NOT for integers.

words.name: shl
words.description: Left shift an integer by the specified number of bits.

words.name: shr
words.description: Right shift an integer by the specified number of bits.
Compare two values for equality. Vectors and lists compare structurally.
Compare two values for inequality. Vectors and lists compare structurally.
Check numeric or string ordering: whether the next value is less than the top value.
Check numeric or string ordering: whether the next value is greater than the top value.
Check numeric or string ordering: whether the next value is less than or equal to the top value.
Check numeric or string ordering: whether the next value is greater than or equal to the top value.

words.name: exec
words.description: Execute a symbol, call node, or quotation.

words.name: i
words.description: Alias for exec. Execute a symbol, call node, or quotation.

words.name: if
words.description: Execute the callable when the condition is true.

words.name: ifelse
words.description: Execute one of two callables based on the condition.

words.name: while
words.description: Repeat while the condition callable yields true.

words.name: cond
words.description: Evaluate predicate/body clause pairs and execute the body for the first true predicate.

words.name: try
words.description: Execute body and run handler if body reports an error.

words.name: error
words.description: Raise a runtime error using a string message.

words.name: app2
words.description: Apply a callable independently to two stack items.

words.name: infra
words.description: Execute a callable using a vector as a temporary stack and collect the resulting stack into a vector.

words.name: cleave
words.description: Apply several callables independently to the same value and leave all outputs on the stack.

words.name: construct
words.description: Apply several callables independently to the same value and collect their outputs into one vector.

words.name: replicate
words.description: Execute a callable n times and collect exactly one result from each execution into a vector.

words.name: times
words.description: Execute a callable a fixed number of times, threading the current stack through each run.

words.name: dip
words.description: Execute a callable while hiding the top item of the stack.

words.name: keep
words.description: Execute a callable while keeping a copy of the top item on the stack.

words.name: bi
words.description: Apply two callables independently to the same stack item.

words.name: linrec
words.description: Linear recursion scheme: run then when pred is true, otherwise run rec1, recurse, then run rec2.

words.name: binrec
words.description: Binary recursion scheme: run then when pred is true, otherwise run rec1, recurse over two produced values, then run rec2.

words.name: genrec
words.description: General recursion scheme: run then when pred is true, otherwise run before, recurse, then run after.

words.name: treerec
words.description: Recursively transform a tree using leaf and node callables.

words.name: each
words.description: Execute a callable for each item in a vector or list, or each byte in a string, threading the current stack through each run.

words.name: map
words.description: Apply a callable to each sequence item and preserve the input sequence family.

words.name: fold
words.description: Reduce a vector, list, or string from left to right by applying a callable to the accumulator and each item.

words.name: filter
words.description: Keep sequence items for which a predicate callable returns true.

words.name: some
words.description: Check whether any sequence item satisfies a predicate callable.

words.name: all
words.description: Check whether every sequence item satisfies a predicate callable.

words.name: split
words.stackEffect: vector|list|string callable -- true false | string sep -- vector
words.description: Partition a vector, list, or string with a predicate callable, or split a string using a separator.

words.name: merge
words.description: Merge two sorted sequences of the same type. The predicate callable runs in a stack sandbox and must leave a boolean on top.

words.name: >vector
words.description: Build a vector from a sequence, preserving order.

words.name: >list
words.description: Build a list from a sequence, preserving order.

words.name: >string
words.description: Build a string from a sequence of one-character strings.

words.name: >deque
words.description: Build a deque from a vector, list, or string, preserving front-to-back order.

words.name: at
words.description: Fetch a vector element or one-character string by index.

words.name: set-at
words.stackEffect: vector idx value -- vector | string idx char -- string
words.description: Return a collection with one element or character replaced.

words.name: slice
words.description: Return a sub-vector or substring from start up to, but not including, end.

words.name: take
words.description: Return the first n items or characters from a vector, list, or string.

words.name: skip
words.description: Return a vector, list, or string with the first n items or characters removed.

words.name: len
words.description: Get the length of a vector, list, string, map, set, deque, or priority queue.

words.name: concat
words.stackEffect: vector vector -- vector | list list -- list | string string -- string
words.description: Concatenate two vectors, two lists, or two strings.

words.name: reverse
words.description: Return a sequence with its items or bytes in reverse order.

words.name: split-mid
words.description: Split a vector, list, or string into left and right halves.

words.name: range
words.description: Create a vector of integers from start up to, but not including, end.

words.name: empty?
words.description: Check whether a vector, list, string, map, set, deque, or priority queue is empty.

words.name: first
words.description: Consume a non-empty ordered collection and return its first item.

words.name: last
words.description: Consume a non-empty ordered collection and return its last item.

words.name: rest
words.description: Get all sequence items except the first one.

words.name: uncons
words.description: Destructure a sequence into its head and tail.

words.name: cons
words.stackEffect: x vector|list -- vector|list | char string -- string
words.description: Add one item to the front of a vector or list, or one-byte string to the front of a string.

words.name: push-front
words.description: Return a deque with item added at the front.

words.name: push-back
words.description: Return a sequence or deque with one item added at the back. String items are one-byte strings.

words.name: pop-front
words.description: Return the updated deque followed by the removed front item.

words.name: pop-back
words.description: Return the updated vector or deque followed by the removed back item.

words.name: contains?
words.stackEffect: vector|list item -- bool | string substring -- bool
words.description: Check item membership in a vector/list or substring presence in a string.

words.name: index-of
words.stackEffect: vector|list item -- idx|-1 | string substring -- idx|-1
words.description: Return the first item or substring index, or -1 when absent.

words.name: unique
words.description: Remove duplicate sequence items, preserving the first occurrence and input family.

words.name: sort
words.description: Sort numbers or strings in natural ascending order, preserving the input family.

words.name: join
words.description: Join a vector or list of strings using a separator.

words.name: trim
words.description: Remove leading and trailing whitespace from a string.

words.name: upper
words.description: Convert a string to uppercase.

words.name: lower
words.description: Convert a string to lowercase.

words.name: char?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte string.

words.name: >char
words.description: Construct a one-character string from an integer code between 0 and 255.

words.name: char-code
words.description: Return the unsigned integer code of a one-character string.

words.name: letter?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte alphabetic string.

words.name: digit?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte digit string.

words.name: alnum?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte alphabetic or digit string.

words.name: space?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte whitespace string.

words.name: upper?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte uppercase string.

words.name: lower?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte lowercase string.

words.name: punct?
words.description: Check whether the input is a one-byte punctuation string.

words.name: >map
words.description: Build a map from a vector or list of two-item key/value pairs. Duplicate keys are an error.

words.name: >set
words.description: Build a set from a vector, list, or string, collapsing duplicate items.

words.name: has?
words.stackEffect: map key -- bool | set item -- bool
words.description: Check whether a map contains a key or a set contains an item.

words.name: get
words.description: Fetch a map value. Missing keys are runtime errors; use get-or or has? when absence is expected.

words.name: get-or
words.description: Fetch a map value, returning the supplied default when the key is absent.

words.name: assoc
words.description: Return a map with key associated to value.

words.name: dissoc
words.description: Return a map without key. Missing keys leave the map unchanged.

words.name: keys
words.description: Return map keys in insertion order.

words.name: values
words.description: Return map values in insertion order.

words.name: pairs
words.description: Return map key/value pairs in insertion order or priority/value pairs in priority order.

words.name: items
words.description: Return set items in insertion order or deque items from front to back.

words.name: insert
words.description: Return a set containing item. An existing item leaves the set and its insertion order unchanged.

words.name: remove
words.description: Return a set without item. Missing items leave the set unchanged.

words.name: union
words.description: Return items from either set, preserving left order then unseen right items.

words.name: intersection
words.description: Return items present in both sets, preserving left-set order.

words.name: difference
words.description: Return items from the left set that are absent from the right set.

words.name: symmetric-difference
words.description: Return left-only items followed by right-only items.

words.name: subset?
words.description: Check whether the left set is a subset of the right set, including equality.

words.name: proper-subset?
words.description: Check whether the left set is a strict subset of the right set.

words.name: superset?
words.description: Check whether the left set is a superset of the right set, including equality.

words.name: proper-superset?
words.description: Check whether the left set is a strict superset of the right set.

words.name: disjoint?
words.description: Check whether the sets have no items in common.

words.name: >pqueue
words.description: Build a priority queue from two-item [priority value] or (priority value) pairs. Lower priorities are returned first.

words.name: pq-push
words.description: Return a priority queue with value inserted at a finite numeric priority.

words.name: pq-peek
words.description: Leave a priority queue unchanged and copy its next priority/value pair to the top.

words.name: pq-pop
words.description: Return the updated priority queue followed by the removed priority and value.

words.name: type-of
words.description: Push a string naming the input type.

words.name: bool?
words.description: Check whether the input is a boolean.

words.name: int?
words.description: Check whether the input is an integer.

words.name: float?
words.description: Check whether the input is a float.

words.name: string?
words.description: Check whether the input is a string.

words.name: symbol?
words.description: Check whether the input is a symbol.

words.name: call?
words.description: Check whether the input is an executable call node.

words.name: vector?
words.description: Check whether the input is a vector.

words.name: list?
words.description: Check whether the input is a linked list.

words.name: map?
words.description: Check whether the input is a map.

words.name: set?
words.description: Check whether the input is a set.

words.name: deque?
words.description: Check whether the input is a deque.

words.name: pqueue?
words.description: Check whether the input is a priority queue.

words.name: number?
words.description: Check whether the input is an integer or float.

words.name: sequence?
words.description: Check whether the input can be iterated as a sequence: vector, list, or string.

words.name: callable?
words.description: Check whether the input can be executed now: a vector quotation, symbol, or call naming a defined word.

words.name: def
words.description: Bind a symbol to a block definition.

words.name: word?
words.description: Check whether a symbol names a dictionary word.

words.name: var?
words.description: Check whether a symbol names a visible dynamic capture.

words.name: body
words.description: Push the quotation body for a user-defined word.

words.name: >symbol
words.description: Convert a string or call node to a symbol.

words.name: >call
words.description: Convert a symbol to an executable call node for runtime quotation construction.

words.name: name
words.description: Convert a symbol to a string.

words.name: words
words.description: Push a sorted vector of dictionary word names as symbols.

words.name: see
words.description: Push a source-like representation of a word definition.

words.name: doc
words.description: Push documentation for a defined word as a string.

words.name: search-words
words.description: Push a sorted vector of symbols whose names or docs match query.

words.name: repr
words.description: Return a source-style representation of a value, with string bytes escaped.

words.name: printf
words.description: Print one value without a newline, interpreting {} placeholders only when the top value is a format string.

words.name: print
words.description: Print one value literally, followed by a newline.
Print the top stack item.

words.name: .s
words.description: Print the current stack state.

words.name: .S
words.description: Print the current stack using source-style value formatting.

words.name: read-key
words.description: Read one byte from input as a one-character string.

words.name: read-line
words.description: Read one line from stdin without its trailing newline.

words.name: clear
words.description: Clear the terminal screen.

words.name: load
words.description: Load and execute a Toy source file in the current context.

words.name: read-file
words.description: Read the entire content of a file into a string.

words.name: write-file
words.description: Write string content to a file.

words.name: delete-file
words.description: Delete a file.

words.name: read-lines
words.description: Read a file and return a vector of its lines.

words.name: file-exists?
words.description: Check if a file exists.

words.name: argc
words.description: Push the number of script arguments.

words.name: argv
words.description: Push script arguments as a vector of strings.

words.name: env?
words.description: Check whether an environment variable is set.

words.name: get-env
words.description: Read an environment variable. Fails when it is not set; use env? when absence is expected.

words.name: set-env
words.description: Set an environment variable, failing if the operation is rejected.

words.name: pwd
words.description: Push the current working directory.

words.name: shell
words.description: Run a shell command and push its output followed by its exit status.

words.name: exit
words.description: Terminate the interpreter.

words.name: sleep
words.description: Pause execution for a number of milliseconds.

words.name: unix-time
words.description: Push whole seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC.

words.name: local-time
words.description: Push the current local year, month, day, hour, minute, and second.

words.name: utc-time
words.description: Push the current UTC year, month, day, hour, minute, and second.

words.name: cpu-time
words.description: Push process CPU time in seconds as a float.

words.name: monotonic-ns
words.description: Push a monotonic wall-clock reading in integer nanosecond units; actual resolution is platform-dependent.

replWords.name: help
replWords.description: Show the categorized REPL word catalog.

replWords.name: trace
replWords.description: Toggle REPL stack display after successful input.
